content:
# elizaos/elizaos.github.io Weekly Report (May 3 - 9, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
Development activity for the week of May 3–9, 2026, was focused on refining the project's configuration management infrastructure. The primary technical effort centered on improving environment variable handling to enhance the modularity of the framework. While development was concentrated on a single configuration-related pull request, the week reflects a steady focus on maintaining the project's technical standards.

## 🛠️ Key Developments
The week’s development was dedicated to infrastructure and configuration improvements:

- **Configuration Management:**
    - The project team initiated work to improve environment variable handling within the codebase. This effort is represented by **[#248](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/pull/248)**, which introduces the `ImportMetaENV` interface to `next.config.js`. This update aims to standardize how environment variables are managed, supporting the project's goal of maintaining a robust and extensible framework. This pull request remains open and is currently awaiting review.

## 🐛 Issues & Triage
- **Closed Issues:** No issues were closed during this reporting period.
- **New & Active Issues:** No new issues were opened, and there were no active issues reported for this week. The project currently has no outstanding issue-based blockers identified in the daily logs.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
Collaboration during this period was focused on the review process for the configuration update. As **[#248](https://github.com/elizaos/elizaos.github.io/pull/248)** is currently awaiting review, the community is engaged in the standard quality assurance workflow for the project. No other significant discussions or community-wide activities were recorded for this timeframe.
